response:
<<A RESEARCH ASSISTANT IS DESIGNING AN EXPERIMENT TO TEST THE EFFECTIVENESS OF A NEW DRUG ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE>>  
<<THE ASSISTANT COLLECTS DATA FROM A SAMPLE OF 50 PATIENTS, RECORDING THEIR BLOOD PRESSURE BEFORE AND AFTER ADMINISTERING THE DRUG>>  
<<THE DATA COLLECTED IS AS FOLLOWS>>  
<<PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURE: X_1, X_2, ..., X_50>>  
<<POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURE: Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_50>>  

<<ASSUME THAT THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES X_i ARE NORMALLY DISTRIBUTED WITH MEAN MU_X AND VARIANCE SIGMA_X^2>>  
<<AND THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES Y_i ARE NORMALLY DISTRIBUTED WITH MEAN MU_Y AND VARIANCE SIGMA_Y^2>>  
<<LET THE COVARIANCE BETWEEN X_i AND Y_i BE SIGMA_XY>>  

<<1. DERIVE THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS (MLEs) FOR THE PARAMETERS MU_X, MU_Y, SIGMA_X^2, SIGMA_Y^2, AND SIGMA_XY GIVEN THE DATA>>  

<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS ARE AS FOLLOWS>>  
<<THE MLE FOR MU_X IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF X_i>>  
<<THE MLE FOR MU_Y IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF Y_i>>  
<<THE MLE FOR SIGMA_X^2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF X_i>>  
<<THE MLE FOR SIGMA_Y^2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF Y_i>>  
<<THE MLE FOR SIGMA_XY IS THE SAMPLE COVARIANCE BETWEEN X_i AND Y_i>>  

<<2. TO TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT THE DRUG HAS A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE, FORMULATE AND CONDUCT A HYPOTHESIS TEST USING THE DERIVED MLEs>>  

<<THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE>>  
<<THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE>>  
<<THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE T-
